content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901         322-6441


July 18, 1984




John T. Hourigan
Delano Professional Building
921 - 13th Avenue
Delano, CA 93215

Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-163

Dear Mr. Hourigan:

Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Mr. Van
Vangel.

FACTS

Mr. Vangel is the president of the Board of Directors of the
North Kern-South Tulare Hospital District. Mr. Vangel's wife is
employed by a doctor as a medical transcriber. Recently, the
Board decided to purchase a CT Scanner, a radiography device,
which will be leased to a partnership of 25 doctors under a
lease/purchase agreement. The doctor employing Mr. Vangel's
wife is one of the 25 partners. Mr. Vangel abstained from
the decision on the purchase of the Scanner.

QUESTION PRESENTED

How will the Political Reform Act affect Mr. Vangel's future
decisionmaking regarding the Scanner?

CONCLUSION

The Act will require Mr. Vangel to disqualify himself on a
decision concerning the Scanner if it will have a material
financial effect on the doctor employing Mr. Vangel's wife.

John T. Hourigan
July 18, 1984
Page 2

DISCUSSION

Government Code Section 87100 1/ prohibits a public
official from making, participating in the making, or in any way
attempting to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows, or has reason to know,
he has a financial interest. An official has a "financial
interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect,2/ on:

* * *

(c) Any source of income . . . aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to . . the public official within 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made....

Section 87103.

The doctor employing Mr. Vangel's wife is a source of
income to the director. (Section 82030.) Mr. Vangel must
disqualify himself from any decision, including a decision
concerning the Scanner, if it will have a material
financial effect on the doctor. If Mr. Vangel is unable to
determine whether to disqualify himself on a particular
decision, he can contact our office for advice.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:plh
Enclosure

_________________

1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

2/ For an explanation of the term "material financial
effect," see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702.

Law Offices of

John T. Hourigan

DALE PARHAM
ASSOCIATE

June 13, 1984

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA        95814

Re: CONFLICT OF INTEREST LETTER OPINION

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Van Vangel, president of the Board of Directors of the North Kern -
South Tulare Hospital District, has requested an opinion from the
Fair Political Practices Commission as to his participation as a
director under the following circumstances:

     Director Vangel has been on the Board in excess of 12 years.
He married his present wife 8 years ago.  She is a former registered
nurse and has hourly contract employment as a medical transcriber/
secretary with a general surgeon within the Hospital District area.
Her time averages 12 to 20 hours per week and she earns well in excess
of the $250.  She had this job when she married Mr. Vangel and he
was already a director when they married.

     The District does not have a hospital and has accumulated funds
well in excess of $1,000,000.  The District proposes to purchase a
CT Scanner, a radiography device, and to lease it to a partnership
formed and made up for that purpose of some 25 doctors from the staff
of the local community hospital.  Most of the partners are doctors
and by far the greater percentage are specialists who welcome the
scanner in their practice with the local hospital.  The District was
formed at a time when the District had little medical and no hospital
facilities available and the fortuitous opening of a large private
hospital in which they have taken active interest has provided the only
hospital/medical attention in North Kern-South Tulare counties.  A
large number of its patients are MediCal, MediCare and welfare.

     The District proposes to purchase and make a lease/purchase agree-
ment with the foregoing partnership.  The contract employer of the
president's wife is one of these 25 partners.  The partnership was
formed for the sole purpose of employing this scanner for their patients,
the patients of the local hospital and the entire medical community.
The doctors are all lessees and each guarantees payment of the entire
group as partners.  Projected earnings apparently will do little more
than pay for the scanner, but scanning is now available only from travel-
ing units which come periodically from San Francisco and/or Bakersfield.
The scanner will, in all probability, resort to a substantial medical

Delano Professional Building
921-13th Avenue
Delano, California 93215
Telephone (805) 725-3522

-2-

Re: Conflict of Interest Letter Opinion

cost to the Delano community.     It would appear to be more of a
convenience for the doctors.     A doctor's financial impact on their
practice is difficult to predict since it is a service that would be
supplied at any rate to them at a slightly higher cost, which would
be passed on to the patients.     Nevertheless, there is a possibility
of profit to the partners as well as loss, depending upon the use made
of the scanner.     The employer/doctor/owner of the scanner will
apparently have little effect on this overall income.  This would not,
in any way, be reflected on the salary of a medical transcriber/typist
(wife of the director).

The question is as to whether Director Van Vangel can participate in the
public hearing and voting at the meeting where the lease/purchase contract
is made.

Scheduled meeting date is July 3, 1984 at 8:00 p.m.     I appreciate the
information you gave me over the phone this morning (Tuesday) and would
appreciate an opinion to this question at your early convenience.

Yours truly,

John T. Hourigan
JOHN T. HOURIGAN
Attorney for the District

JTH:mm

Dictated by the writer;
signed and mailed in his
absence to avoid delay.

John T. Hourigan
